page: Microsoft Azure B12ms is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the BS family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The configuration includes 12 vCPUs and 12 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core. It is equipped with 48.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 98 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance type.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance split between single-core and multi-core operations. Single-core performance is consistently weak, falling into the bottom 25% for stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded compression tasks like gzip and bzip3. Conversely, multi-core and parallelized workloads perform at an average level, placing in the middle 50% for stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, software compilation, and ray tracing. Memory bandwidth tests also show average performance, with cached read speeds at 1079.46 GB/sec and uncached write speeds at 34.47 GB/sec.

Due to its burstable CPU allocation model, the B12ms offers a cost-efficient profile for workloads with variable CPU utilization. The primary tradeoff is the performance limitation under sustained, heavy single-threaded CPU loads, as evidenced by its weak single-core benchmarks. However, with 48.0 GB of RAM and average multi-core capabilities, it is suitable for database operations, static web serving, and Redis caching, where resource demands fluctuate and multi-threaded throughput is prioritized over continuous peak single-core processing.
description: Microsoft Azure B12ms is an x86_64 burstable virtual machine from the BS server family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features 12 vCPUs, 12 physical cores, 48.0 GB of RAM, and 98 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Benchmark results indicate a clear performance tradeoff: single-core CPU execution and single-threaded compression tasks are weak, placing in the bottom 25%, while multi-core operations, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform at an average level within the middle 50%. The burstable CPU allocation provides a cost-efficient resource model for variable workloads rather than sustained high-compute tasks. This configuration is suitable for database operations, Redis caching, and static web serving where resource demands fluctuate.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B12ms is a burstable x86_64 virtual machine featuring 12 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 48 GB of RAM, and 98 GB of local storage, offering average multi-core performance for variable database and web workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure B12ms is a burstable x86_64 virtual machine with 12 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 48 GB RAM, and 98 GB local storage for variable workloads.
tagline: A burstable x86_64 virtual machine combining twelve Intel Xeon cores with forty-eight gigabytes of memory for variable multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 12 vCPUs and 12 physical cores
Equipped with 48.0 GB of system memory and 98 GB of local storage
Burstable CPU allocation model designed for variable, non-sustained workloads
Average multi-core performance in software compilation and database operations
Weak single-core execution speeds across compression and processing benchmarks
Best suited for database operations, Redis caching, and static web serving
